layman_headline: Fear grips the market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with most coins pulling back. Bitcoin is holding near $64K but altcoins are getting hit harder, with BTC dominance at 56.4% squeezing alternative coins. While Ethereum's RSI is approaching oversold territory (a historically reliable signal), the overall chart structure is still bearish and there's no clear catalyst to trigger a reversal yet. The smartest move right now is to wait for a better entry rather than chase into weakness.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch for Ethereum to dip toward the $1,700-$1,780 support zone. If it holds there with increasing volume and RSI drops below 30, that would be a much higher-conviction entry. Don't rush — fear markets reward patience.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling entry right now. DEXE has a strong chart bias (+1.10) but RSI at 85 is dangerously overbought with a 0.33x risk/reward — a classic trap. HYPE has a bullish chart (+1.10) but has dropped 11% in 7 days and RSI at 59 isn't oversold enough for a bounce trade. BONK's RSI at 33 is approaching oversold but the chart bias is deeply bearish (-1.40) with a Bearish Marubozu — catching falling knives in memecoins during fear markets is poor risk management. ETH at RSI 35 is the most interesting setup given our signal credibility data showing RSI has an 80% win rate, but the chart bias is neutral (0.0), price is below all MAs, and geopolitical risks (Iran strikes pressuring BTC) make this premature. I want to see RSI drop to 30 or a bullish MACD cross before committing capital.
risk_factors: Iran-related geopolitical escalation could drive BTC below $60K, dragging all alts significantly lower
BTC dominance at 56.4% and rising means capital is flowing out of alts — fighting this trend is dangerous
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear (sub-20) where historically the best entries occur
watch_list: ETH RSI dropping below 30 and/or price testing the $1,700 support with a bullish reversal candle
BTC price action around $62,500 — a breakdown below could trigger a broader alt selloff creating better entries
Fear & Greed index — if it drops to sub-20 (extreme fear) with ETH holding $1,700, that's a high-conviction setup
market_context: The market is in a 'Fear' state (27/100) with BTC holding near $64K but under pressure from geopolitical tensions. BTC dominance at 56.4% is squeezing altcoins, making this a poor environment for alt entries until conditions stabilize.
context_banner: Fear markets reward patience — the best swing entries come from waiting for oversold RSI readings at strong support levels, not chasing into weakness.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't reached the sub-30 zone that would trigger a high-conviction entry.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) is improving from 25 but hasn't reached extreme fear territory where contrarian entries historically perform best.
Bitcoin price sagging under $62.5K due to Iran strikes adding pressure to risk assets — a headwind for any new long positions in the near term.
ETH derivatives reset signals potential setup for next retail trade, but timing is premature given macro headwinds.
Regulatory uncertainty persists as Clarity Act passage odds hit record lows — structural headwind for crypto risk appetite.
